message:
feat(types,cx): add SystemPressure type and Cx integration for compute budget propagation

Introduce a new lock-free SystemPressure abstraction that enables
advisory degradation signaling across the async runtime. This is
foundational infrastructure for cooperative load-shedding: components
can query a shared headroom value (0.0-1.0) via atomic f64 to decide
whether to shed optional work without requiring any locks or blocking.

Changes:
- src/types/pressure.rs (NEW): Atomic SystemPressure state struct with
  lock-free headroom getter/setter clamped to [0.0, 1.0], designed for
  high-frequency reads from hot paths with minimal contention
- src/types/mod.rs: Register and re-export the new pressure module so
  it is available as types::pressure::SystemPressure
- src/cx/cx.rs: Thread SystemPressure through Cx via an optional
  Arc<SystemPressure> field, with_pressure() builder method, and
  pressure() accessor -- plumbed through Clone and all constructors so
  child contexts inherit the pressure handle from their parent
- src/lib.rs: Update top-level re-exports for the new module

The design is intentionally advisory and non-blocking: producers set
headroom from a monitoring loop, consumers read it opportunistically.
No component is forced to act on pressure -- it is a cooperative hint
that enables graceful degradation under load.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
